item.title: Business Brief: One neat trick to stop car theft and fentanyl trade
item.description: The solution is simple, inexpensive, and would generate revenue

item.title: Jamie Dimon warns private credit losses will be larger than feared
item.description: JPMorgan chief raises alarm on weakening lending standards in annual shareholder letter

item.title: An Economist’s Quest to Solve America’s Wage Problem
item.description: Arindrajit Dube argues that the answer is empowering workers and setting mandatory wage standards across industries.

item.title: Quebecor’s Freedom Mobile is enjoying a newfound status
item.description: The telecom carrier is taking advantage of a federal policy that gives it access to rivals’ networks and expires in 2030

item.title: Why doesn’t Ontario want its pension funds to manage its new investment agency?
item.description: Ontario’s taxpayers deserve answers on how the government intends to spend their money

item.title: FirstFT: US to unleash ‘hell’ if Iran doesn’t reopen Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Private equity buyouts slump and Cuba’s solar tech lifeline

item.title: A wave of cheap Chinese EVs is poised to upend Canada’s auto industry
item.description: The post A wave of cheap Chinese EVs is poised to upend Canada’s auto industry appeared first on The Logic .

item.title: New U.S.-Iran ceasefire proposal is shared as air strikes kill more than 25
item.description: Head of intelligence for Iran’s paramilitary Revolutionary Guard killed in attack

item.title: U.S. scaling back detention of goods made with forced labour as it probes other countries’ records
item.description: Review of Chinese trade statistics, recent U.S. enforcement record suggest Trump administration faces gaps in its own approach

item.title: How to fight car thefts, fentanyl and lift our economy – all at once
item.description: Unlike in other countries, shipping manifest data is a black hole in Canada – the public does not know what goes in and out

item.title: Broken CN Rail bridge at Vancouver port saps energy superpower ambitions
item.description: The Second Narrows Rail Bridge plays a critical role in shipping at Canada’s biggest and busiest port

item.title: Fast fashion’s offcuts are fighting back
item.description: Squeezing money out of a legacy brand may be better than trying to build a new one

item.title: US employment: more sludge
item.description: Plus, guns and butter redux

item.title: Why this oil shock is different
item.description: Governments and central banks are out of policy ammunition to contain the economic fallout

item.title: Trump family-linked investment group backs spa company offering medical screening
item.description: XWell plans to roll out AI-powered infectious disease tool at airports during World Cup

item.title: Russian crypto payment system expands into Africa
item.description: Videos show A7 office in Nigeria, while company also claims new branch in Zimbabwe

item.title: Boring stocks are still beating the herd
item.description: Index of under-reported UK equities has done better than the FTSE 100

item.title: Ukraine’s drones dent Russia’s war-fuelled oil windfall
item.description: Disrupted exports add pressure to markets already reeling from Iran conflict

item.title: Private equity buyouts slump as AI fears and war dent dealmaking
item.description: Groups agreed acquisitions worth $172bn in three months to March, a 36 per cent fall from previous quarter

item.title: Stefano Pessina vows to rebuild ‘all the value’ lost at his Boots empire
item.description: Italian billionaire hails his wife’s turnaround of the pharmacy chain in his first interview since its struggling US owner was taken private

item.title: Power-starved Cuba deepens reliance on Chinese solar tech
item.description: Imports of solar technology have soared, offering some respite for country that now faces near-total oil blockade

item.title: AI has arrived in auditing. Are regulators ready?
item.description: The technology is already leading to rapid changes in the way company accounts are reviewed

item.title: China and Europe launch rare joint space mission
item.description: Solar-focused project seeks to understand geomagnetic storms that can disrupt terrestrial communications

item.title: Deutsche Bank chief earns bumper share returns as rebound bet pays off
item.description: Christian Sewing holds stock worth €15mn and another €25mn in unvested shares after turnaround fuels share price rally

item.title: Chinese micro-dramas target US with familiar tropes for phone audience
item.description: Love and fantasy stories dominate a wildly popular genre in China that is trying to adapt to western tastes

item.title: World’s biggest battery maker takes ambitions to the high seas
item.description: China’s CATL wants to electrify global shipping fleets but hurdles remain to large-scale adoption

item.title: Neurocrine nears $2.5bn-plus deal for biotech behind obesity disorder treatment
item.description: Soleno Therapeutics makes first commercialised drug for extreme hunger caused by Prader-Willi syndrome

item.title: AI dolls offer companionship to the elderly
item.description: South Korea’s strained social care system turns to ChatGPT-enabled devices as population ages

item.title: The current market volatility is a good test for DIY investors
item.description: If you’re having a hard time right now you might need to reconsider your choice of managing your own accounts

item.description: Iran continues to be a major flashpoint for investors

item.title: Iran allows Iraqi ships through Hormuz strait
item.description: Tehran’s decision could allow 3mn b/d of oil to be released to international markets

item.title: Is AI the new fracking?
item.description: The backlash against data centres chimes with energy Nimbyism of the past

item.title: Feeling stressed? Get a workplace bestie
item.description: In a moment when stress feels like a constant, the simplest solution might also be the most human, finding your person at work

item.title: How Trump became tech’s regulator-in-chief
item.description: His interventions in the sector exceed anything the EU has done

item.title: How big an impact will the war have on US inflation?
item.description: Market Questions is the FT’s guide to the week ahead

item.title: All that glisters: Maga influencers promote gold but investors feel short-changed
item.description: Marketing pitches tell retirees the precious metal is a safe harbour amid claims of misrepresentation and exorbitant fees

item.title: Nelson Peltz’s bidding war highlights $25bn wave of asset manager consolidation
item.description: Quest for scale puts global money manager tie-ups on pace to crush last year’s deal total as costs and competition mount

item.title: ‘Lights are blinking’: LaGuardia crash raises alarm over US aviation safety
item.description: Fatal Air Canada Express collision highlights years of under-investment in ageing air traffic control system

item.title: Why cutting student grants may have long-term financial implications for young graduates
item.description: Debt can impact life choices such as careers and which city you live in

item.title: Why McDonald’s and KFC are growing like wildfire in China
item.description: Western fast-food brands are expanding into the countryside

item.title: Inflation or recession? The tug of war in bond markets
item.description: Governments’ borrowing costs are being pulled in opposite directions

item.title: AI and tech companies drive Hong Kong listings to 5-year high
item.description: Primary and secondary offerings raise more than $13bn in first quarter of 2026

item.title: Campaign to curb cars in Berlin sparks uproar ahead of election
item.description: Conservatives and far right are opposing a citizens’ initiative to restrict traffic in the German capital

item.title: Countries must not hoard fuel during Iran war, warns IEA
item.description: Fatih Birol makes veiled reference to China as he urges nations to avoid export bans despite worsening supply shock

item.title: The federal government faces calls to begin enriching uranium. Should it?
item.description: Move would have significant implications for Saskatchewan’s uranium industry, power producers and Canada’s international standing

item.description: Plus, the gas price shock isn’t hitting Canadian cities evenly

item.title: U.S. rescues missing pilot from fighter jet shot down by Iran
item.description: Fighter jet was the first U.S. aircraft to crash in Iranian territory since conflict began

item.title: Air Canada CEO’s premature exit shows language is still identity in Quebec
item.description: Michael Rousseau’s English-only statement on New York plane crash caused controversy that led to his retirement

item.title: Go to court and lose out on £9bn car finance redress scheme, says FCA boss
item.description: Comments by Nikhil Rathi position watchdog for stand-off with claims management companies and law firms

item.title: Trump’s tales are still muddling markets
item.description: Continuation of the Middle East war is sapping hopes of a return to more normal conditions after the conflict

item.title: For Easter-egg makers, it is chocolate tomorrow
item.description: Sweeter times lie ahead as the market for cocoa buyers improves but prices take time to feed through the chain

item.title: Fintechs race to lure customers away from UK’s high-street banks
item.description: Wise announced plans to roll out current accounts following Klarna’s pivot to become a full-service bank last year

item.title: Why investment trusts are going big on private equity
item.description: Trusts give retail investors the opportunity to get a slice of highly sought-after private companies — but the trend poses difficult questions about valuation and performance

item.title: Middle East war day 35 as it happened: US launches rescue operation for plane’s missing crew member
item.description: Second aircraft lost near Strait of Hormuz after one of two aboard F-15 is found

item.title: Montreal Port Authority CEO abruptly leaves role
item.description: No reason was given for the departure of Julie Gascon

item.title: In the Iran disaster, Trump’s only choice is a recession or a worse recession
item.description: Whether the President likes it or not, the U.S. is integrated with the global oil market

item.title: Canada, China sign pledge in Beijing to deepen financial-sector ties
item.description: Canada’s Finance Minister says he raised the importance of labour standards during meetings with top Chinese officials

item.title: Starmer’s cost of living tsar calls for rethink on fuel duty rise
item.description: Comments by Richard Walker come as UK motorists face £2-a-litre diesel as Iran war drives wholesale prices to 4-year high

item.title: U.S. economy adds 178,000 jobs in March, unemployment rate dips to 4.3%
item.description: Hiring rebounded from a loss of 133,000 jobs in February, with health care leading job growth

item.title: Rachel Reeves’ ‘mansion tax’ to hit about 160,000 homes, says fiscal watchdog
item.description: OBR analysis estimates one-fifth of affected property owners will appeal revaluations Tax
